vendorUiGzip.label: vendor-ui gzip budget
vendorUiGzip.rationale: Shared UI/vendor surface is allowed to stay large because it amortizes across every browser lane entrypoint, but regressions here should still be noticeable.

initialJsGzip.label: initial JS gzip budget
initialJsGzip.rationale: Initial JS stays below the current desktop shell bootstrap envelope so login, settings, and shell boot gates do not quietly drift upward.

objectsPageGzip.label: ObjectsPage gzip budget
objectsPageGzip.rationale: Objects is the heaviest task-completion surface; optional overlays already lazy-load, so the remaining tree/list/details orchestration sets a slightly higher stable floor for this route.

uploadsPageGzip.label: UploadsPage gzip budget
uploadsPageGzip.rationale: Uploads remains intentionally tight because it should stay a thin entrypoint over shared transfer/runtime code, but the stable floor now includes the route's selection preview and source-sheet presentation.

transfersGzip.label: Transfers gzip budget
transfersGzip.rationale: Transfers carries realtime queue and job-event behavior, so the budget is looser than Uploads but still capped to catch accidental UI layering or duplicate runtime logic.
